---
title: "modelling clutch size"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(here)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
library(performance)
library(MASS)
library(pscl)
library(piecewiseSEM)
library(standardize)
#library(semPlot)
library(dplyr)
library(glmmTMB)
```

WARM OR HOT LARVAL TREAMENT
```{r}

results <- read.csv(here("data", "clean data", "results.csv"), colClasses = c(sex = "character"))

#make maternal ID a factor
results$maternalID <- as.factor(results$maternalID)

#make a treatment combination column
results <- results %>%
  mutate(treatment_combination = paste(larval_treatment, pupal_treatment, sep = "_"))  %>%
  mutate()

#number of mated females in each treatment group 
table(results$treatment_combination)

#remove ambient, because these need to be modelled separately. also remove larval treatment of cool and mean, because the sample size isn't big enough to model - just not enough clutches.
#I think I will model the ambient larva treatment separately.
results_filtered <- results %>%
  filter(
    larval_treatment %in% c("warm", "hot")) %>%
  mutate(numerical_pupal_treatment = case_when(
    pupal_treatment == "cool"  ~ -1.5,
    pupal_treatment == "mean"  ~  0.0,
    pupal_treatment == "warm"  ~  1.5,
    pupal_treatment == "hot"   ~  2.5,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )) %>% 
  mutate(numerical_larval_treatment = case_when(
    larval_treatment == "warm"  ~  1.5,
    larval_treatment == "hot"   ~  2.5,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg = weight * 1000) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg_centered = weight_mg - mean(weight_mg, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(across(
    c(weight_mg, egg_mean_1A, clutch_size),
    ~ as.numeric(scale(.)),
    .names = "{.col}_z"
  ))


results_filtered <- results_filtered %>%
  mutate(
    larval_treatment = factor(larval_treatment, levels = c("warm","hot")),
    pupal_treatment = factor(pupal_treatment, levels = c("ambient", "cool","mean","warm","hot"))
  )

#how many eggs were counted in total
sum(results$clutch_size) 

#results <- results %>%
  #filter(clutch_size != 0)

mean(results$clutch_size)

sd(results$clutch_size)

```

```{r}
#look at the distribution of clutch size
ggplot(results, aes(x = clutch_size)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5) +  # change this value to control bin width +
  labs(title = "Distribution of clutch size",
       x = "clutch size",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

# mean
mean(results_filtered$clutch_size, na.rm = TRUE)

# variance
var(results_filtered$clutch_size, na.rm = TRUE)

#the mean is much greater than the variance, suggesting that a negative binomial model might be better than the poisson model


pois_mod <- glm(clutch_size ~ 1, family = poisson(link = "log"), data = results)

nb_mod <- glm.nb(clutch_size ~ 1, data = results)

AIC(pois_mod, nb_mod)
#the AIC is way, way lower for the negative binomial model, so this is the better model to use.






#look at the distribution of female mass
ggplot(results, aes(x = weight)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.005) +  # change this value to control bin width +
  labs(title = "Distribution of female mass",
       x = "female mass",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

#look at the distribution of mean egg sizes
ggplot(results, aes(x = egg_mean_1A)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.01) +  # change this value to control bin width +
  labs(title = "Distribution of mean egg size",
       x = "mean egg size",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```



```{r}
#trying negative binomial models
lm1_nb <- glm.nb(clutch_size ~  larval_treatment, data = results_filtered)
summary(lm1_nb)
AIC(lm1_nb)

lm2_nb <- glm.nb(clutch_size ~  larval_treatment + weight_mg_centered, data = results_filtered)
summary(lm2_nb)
AIC(lm2_nb)

lm3_nb <- glm.nb(clutch_size ~  larval_treatment + pupal_treatment + weight_mg_centered, data = results_filtered)
summary(lm3_nb)
AIC(lm3_nb)

lm4_nb <- glmer.nb(clutch_size ~  larval_treatment + pupal_treatment + weight_mg_centered + (1|maternalID), data = results_filtered)
summary(lm4_nb)
```



```{r}
#now try zero-inflated negative binomial models 
lm1_zinb <- zeroinfl(clutch_size ~  larval_treatment, data = results_filtered, dist = "negbin")
summary(lm1_zinb)
AIC(lm1_zinb)

lm2_zinb <- zeroinfl(clutch_size ~  larval_treatment + weight_mg_centered, data = results_filtered, dist = "negbin")
summary(lm2_zinb)
AIC(lm2_zinb)

lm3_zinb <- zeroinfl(clutch_size ~  larval_treatment + pupal_treatment + weight_mg_centered, data = results_filtered, dist = "negbin")
summary(lm3_zinb)
```

```{r}

mod.hurdl_1 <- glmmTMB(clutch_size ~ larval_treatment, ziformula = ~ larval_treatment, family = truncated_nbinom2, data = results_filtered)
summary(mod.hurdl_1)
AIC(mod.hurdl_1)
#2474

mod.hurdl_2 <- glmmTMB(clutch_size ~ larval_treatment + weight_mg_centered, ziformula = ~ larval_treatment + weight_mg_centered, family = truncated_nbinom2, data = results_filtered)
summary(mod.hurdl_2)
AIC(mod.hurdl_2)
#2466

mod.hurdl_3 <- glmmTMB(clutch_size ~ larval_treatment + weight_mg_centered + pupal_treatment, ziformula = ~ larval_treatment + weight_mg_centered + pupal_treatment, family = truncated_nbinom2, data = results_filtered)
summary(mod.hurdl_3)
AIC(mod.hurdl_3)
#2478


mod.hurdl_4 <- glmmTMB(clutch_size ~ larval_treatment + weight_mg_centered + (1 | maternalID), ziformula = ~ larval_treatment + weight_mg_centered, family = truncated_nbinom2, data = results_filtered)
summary(mod.hurdl_4)
AIC(mod.hurdl_4)
#2467

mod.hurdl_5 <- glmmTMB(clutch_size ~  weight_mg_centered, ziformula = ~ weight_mg_centered, family = truncated_nbinom2, data = results_filtered)
summary(mod.hurdl_5)
AIC(mod.hurdl_5)
#2462


```

```{r}
#model diagnostics for the best model (mod.hurdl_5)
library(DHARMa)

# simulate residuals
sim_res <- simulateResiduals(mod.hurdl_5)

# basic diagnostic plots
plot(sim_res)

testDispersion(sim_res)

testZeroInflation(sim_res)


#plot observed vs predicted
pred <- predict(mod.hurdl_5, type = "response")

plot(pred, results_filtered$clutch_size)
abline(0, 1, col = "red")
```






```{r}
#looking at the effect of larval treatment on female mass
results <- results %>%
  mutate(
    larval_treatment = factor(larval_treatment, levels = c("cool","mean","warm","hot"))
  ) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg = weight * 1000)



wm_1 <- lm(weight_mg ~ larval_treatment, data = results)
summary(wm_1)

AIC(wm_1)
```

AMBIENT LARVAL TREATMENT

```{r}
results_filtered_ambient <- results %>%
  filter(larval_treatment %in% c("wild", "ambient")) %>%
  mutate(numerical_pupal_treatment = case_when(
    pupal_treatment == "cool"  ~ -1.5,
    pupal_treatment == "mean"  ~  0.0,
    pupal_treatment == "warm"  ~  1.5,
    pupal_treatment == "hot"   ~  2.5,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )) %>% 
  mutate(numerical_larval_treatment = case_when(
    larval_treatment == "warm"  ~  1.5,
    larval_treatment == "hot"   ~  2.5,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg = weight * 1000) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg_centered = weight_mg - mean(weight_mg, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(across(
    c(weight_mg, egg_mean_1A, clutch_size),
    ~ as.numeric(scale(.)),
    .names = "{.col}_z"
  ))


results_filtered_ambient <- results_filtered_ambient %>%
  mutate(
    pupal_treatment = factor(pupal_treatment, levels = c("wild", "ambient", "cool","mean","warm","hot")),
    larval_treatment = factor(larval_treatment, levels = c("wild", "ambient")))
```

```{r}

mod.hurdl_1 <- glmmTMB(clutch_size ~ pupal_treatment, ziformula = ~ pupal_treatment, family = truncated_nbinom2, data = results_filtered_ambient)
summary(mod.hurdl_1)
AIC(mod.hurdl_1)
#AIC = 1936

mod.hurdl_2 <- glmmTMB(clutch_size ~ larval_treatment, ziformula = ~ larval_treatment, family = truncated_nbinom2, data = results_filtered_ambient)
summary(mod.hurdl_2)
AIC(mod.hurdl_2)
#1922

#THIS IS THE BEST MODEL
mod.hurdl_3 <- glmmTMB(clutch_size ~ weight_mg_centered, ziformula = ~ weight_mg_centered, family = truncated_nbinom2, data = results_filtered_ambient)
summary(mod.hurdl_3)
AIC(mod.hurdl_3)
#AIC = 1099


mod.hurdl_4 <- glmmTMB(clutch_size ~ weight_mg_centered + (1 | maternalID), ziformula = ~ weight_mg_centered + (1|maternalID), family = truncated_nbinom2, data = results_filtered_ambient)
summary(mod.hurdl_4)
AIC(mod.hurdl_4)
#AIC = 1103

```
